projects.title: Projects

desktopPet.name: DesktopPet — AI Agent Desktop Companion
desktopPet.tagline: Full-cycle AI Agent practice project built with WPF + MiMo AI API
desktopPet.description: DesktopPet is an intelligent desktop pet application featuring the Warframe character Ordis. It implements a complete AI Agent autonomous behavior system — sensing user activity, initiating proactive dialogue, recognizing voice commands, and featuring a unique Glitch personality system.

highlights.agentDesc: Perception (app monitoring/away detection) → Decision (proactive dialogue engine/RAG) → Execution (TTS/Glitch) pipeline
highlights.glitchDesc: Three-layer personality, 10 trigger conditions with independent cooldown management
highlights.eventbusDesc: 11 event types with pub/sub bus, zero direct dependencies between modules
highlights.proactiveDesc: 6 trigger types + RAG-enhanced context for LLM prompt generation
highlights.voiceDesc: Whisper local STT + MiMo TTS (3 voice presets) + Windows TTS fallback
highlights.achievementDesc: Event-driven achievement detection with 3 condition handlers

eshop.name: eShop — Microservices E-commerce Platform
eshop.tagline: Full-featured e-commerce system with Spring Cloud microservices + Vue 2
eshop.description: eShop is an online trading platform for digital products, covering new products, second-hand trading, device recycling, and equipment rental. Built with Spring Cloud microservices architecture, achieving decoupled services for users, products, and orders.

highlights.multicategoryDesc: New product browsing, shopping cart, order management
highlights.usedDesc: Complete ecosystem for used sales, recycling quotes, and transactions
highlights.compareDesc: Up to 4 products JSON parameter comparison with difference highlighting
highlights.authDesc: API Gateway JWT filter + service-level interceptor verification

aiNativeDev.title: AI-Native Dev Workflow: Building Projects with Claude Code
aiNativeDev.summary: From zero to one, completing the DesktopPet project in 2 weeks using Claude Code.
aiNativeDev.content:
In the DesktopPet project, I used Claude Code throughout the entire development process, establishing a complete AI-Native development workflow.

## Why Claude Code

The traditional development flow is: requirements → design → coding → testing → deployment. The core idea of AI-Native development is making AI your pair programming partner, not just a code generator.

## CLAUDE.md Project Config

At the start of each project, I write a CLAUDE.md file containing:
- Project architecture overview
- Tech stack choices and constraints
- Code conventions and naming
- Key design decisions

## ADR Architecture Decision Records

For important architectural decisions, I write ADRs documenting:
- Background and problem
- Options considered
- Final decision and rationale
- Consequences and impact

## Results

With this workflow, DesktopPet was completed in 2 weeks: 190 source files, 12,000+ lines of code, 413 test cases.

desktopPetArch.title: DesktopPet Architecture: EventBus-Driven 8-Module System
desktopPetArch.summary: Deep dive into DesktopPet's event-driven architecture and how EventBus enables zero-coupling communication.
desktopPetArch.content:
DesktopPet uses an EventBus-decoupled 8-module architecture where each module communicates through events, achieving high modularity and extensibility.

## Architecture Overview

The system has three layers:
- Perception: AppMonitor, InputMonitor, AwayDetector
- Decision: ProactiveDialogueService, ContextRetrievalService
- Execution: InteractionService, VoiceInteractionService, EmotionService

## EventBus Design

The EventBus is the central communication hub, supporting:
- 11 event types
- Thread-safe pub/sub
- Exception isolation
- Async event handling

## Glitch Personality System

Ordis's three-layer personality system is one of the most interesting designs. With 10 independent trigger conditions, each with its own probability, cooldown, and check interval, it creates unpredictable yet reasonable glitch personality behavior.
